title: calochortus howellii
intro: Calochortus howellii, also known as Howell's mariposa lily or Howell's fairy bells, is a species of flowering plant in the lily family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The roots and leaves of the Calochortus howellii have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved respiratory health, and enhanced immune function. Herbal preparations of Calochortus howellii typically involve drying and processing the plant material, followed by infusion or decoction in hot water to create teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal use.
